pipeline.detail: This array DRIVES generation: stages execute top to bottom, each exactly once, in ANY order; set active: false to skip a stage (its wall-region classification turns off with it). OVERWRITES the height field with the natural surface — stages ordered before it are discarded. Radial cosine mountain ripple (CENTER bar) plus team-count divider ridges (DIVIDERS bar), faded to flat before the outer map buffer (generation.edgeFadeWidthFraction).

Blends the current height toward the signed PERIMETER bar magnitude: untouched inside generation.perimeter.innerRadiusFraction, cosine-blending to hard override at outerRadiusFraction (negative = round island below water, positive = rim wall).

Snapshots the current surface and estimates its slope at this position in the order; later stages consume both (plateau shelf sizing, waters-edge wall sizing, and the shore-distance estimate |surface - waterLevel| / gradient behind both shoreline fades). Stages that need them BEFORE this stage see zeros: plateau walls go near-vertical and the shoreline fades to no effect.

D-PLATEAU bar snap of the current height onto the TERRAIN_D_TERRAIN lattice; shelf width from generation.plateau.shelfFractionOfStep, walls at the PLATEAU WALL bar slope via the shared ramp curve. Walls are closed-loop WALL TRIS regions.

Team-periodic beach/cliff halves per player slice (beach centered on the player spoke at the BEACH SLOPE bar, 0 = flat shelf at water level; cliff centered on the divider at the W-CLIFF bar height) joined by wall-steep end caps from the PLATEAU WALL slope. Both operators apply fully at the waterline and raised-cosine fade to no effect over generation.shoreline.beachFadeRadius / cliffFadeRadius of horizontal shore distance, following the water's curves on both sides. Cliff wall/cap regions are closed-loop WALL TRIS; far from water they are flattened, not removed.
max(world.floorY, height) at this position in the order; a final safety clamp always runs at the end of the pipeline regardless. The water plane sits at floorY * (1 - water.levelFraction).

Per-deposit circular flat pads (flatPadCells, null = auto-sized to the resource footprint) at their resolved heights, raised-cosine blending back into the current height over each ring's terrainBlendRadius.
